fields.description:
# Thread ref + source mode through the code-reading gates

## Why

Today the gates derive their code root from the MCP process cwd / `repo_root` and read its
mutable working tree (`cfg.repo_path`). They must instead read the snapshot materialized at
the client-specified ref, with a uniform single-`ref` interface across every operation.

## What

- Add `ref` (branch|tag|SHA, default `origin/main`) and `source` (`attested` default |
  `local`) parameters to: `review_plan`, `verify_completion`, `review_ticket`, `review_code`,
  `scan_spec`. Every operation takes ONE ref and gets ONE snapshot (code-review included; no
  base+head pair in this epic).
- Attested: call S1/S2 to obtain a snapshot dir at the pinned SHA; point the gate's file
  tools (`pai_tools` root / `cfg.repo_path`) at the snapshot dir instead of the server cwd.
- Local: read the server's in-place checkout directly (no materialization, no cache); mark
  the run as unsigned (S4 bars signing). In local mode `repo_root` IS the read root (the
  checkout); in attested mode `repo_root` only locates the object DB and the snapshot is the
  read root.
- Replace the implicit `repo_root`-derived code root with the snapshot handle across all three
  dispatch touchpoints: `gate_ops`, `completion.py`, `operations._assemble_context`.
- **Back-compat / expand-contract:** existing callers that pass neither param get the
  documented defaults (ref=`origin/main`, source=`attested`), so the change is additive; state
  this back-out path (defaults preserve today's "verify latest" intent without the wrong-branch bug).
- **Consumer tolerance:** a gate whose snapshot dir is absent/being retired (S2 GC) treats it
  as a miss and re-materializes (ENOENT -> re-acquire), never reads a partial tree.
- **Defaults configurable:** the `ref`/`source` defaults are overridable per deployment, not
  hardcoded constants.

## Scope

- In: parameter threading, gate file-tool re-rooting to the snapshot, local-mode in-place read,
  back-compat defaults, consumer absent/stale tolerance.
- Out: signing rules (S4); CLI/MCP surface schemas (S5); snapshot/cache internals (S1/S2);
  the architecture ADR (authored in S2, cross-referenced here).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Each of the five gates accepts `ref` + `source`; attested reads the snapshot dir, never
      the server's working tree.
- [ ] Omitting the params applies the documented defaults (ref=`origin/main`, source=`attested`);
      the defaults are configurable, not hardcoded.
- [ ] All three dispatch touchpoints (`gate_ops`, `completion.py`,
      `operations._assemble_context`) consume the snapshot handle — not just the interface signature.
- [ ] `local` mode reads the in-place checkout (dirty allowed), is flagged unsigned, and uses
      `repo_root` as the read root; attested uses `repo_root` only to locate the object DB.
- [ ] code-review uses a single snapshot at a single ref (no base+head pair) like the others.
- [ ] A gate tolerates an absent/being-GC'd snapshot dir by re-materializing (ENOENT -> miss),
      never reading a partial tree.
- [ ] Regression test asserts the attested verdict is IDENTICAL regardless of the server's
      checked-out branch (the behavioral contract — NOT an output snapshot / change-detector),
      reproducing this session's wrong-branch false-negative.

## Additional notes + Acceptance Criteria (advisory remediation)

- Terminology: "snapshot dir" and "snapshot handle" refer to the SAME entity (the handle wraps
  the dir + SHA + cleanup); used interchangeably.
- Defaults config mechanism: `ref`/`source` defaults are read through the EXISTING rebar config
  precedence (rebar.toml `[llm]`/gate table + `REBAR_*` env override), consistent with how
  other LLM/gate settings resolve — not a bespoke new mechanism.
- Rollback: `source=local` is the explicit back-out that restores the prior in-place working-
  tree read if the default snapshot read ever regresses a deployment.

- [ ] The `ref`/`source` defaults resolve via the standard rebar config + `REBAR_*` env
      precedence (named, not a deferred mechanism choice).
- [ ] A test covers the ENOENT/absent-snapshot path (gate re-materializes, never reads a partial tree).
- [ ] A test covers local-mode unsigned flagging (a local run is reflected unsigned in the result).
- [ ] `source=local` is documented as the rollback to in-place reads.
